accountMode.single: This account uses a single signature.

multipleSignersInfo: This action requires multiple signatures. Please provide enough secret keys to meet the threshold.

job.title: Create multisig job (Standard)
job.desc: Standard workflow for productive multisig. Creates a job ID and collects signatures in a distributed manner.
job.short: Creates a multisig job (Job-ID) and collects distributed signatures.

infoBody.one: The transaction is stored as a multisig job in the backend and receives a job ID. Nothing is sent directly.
infoBody.two: All subsequent signers open the "Multisig orders" menu item in their SKM instance, load the job, and sign.
infoBody.three: As soon as enough signatures are collected (threshold reached), the transaction is sent or can be sent.
infoBody.four: You can pass the job ID on to other signers, or they can select the job directly in "Multisig orders".

steps.one: Step 1: A multisig job is created and receives a job ID.
steps.two: Step 2: All subsequent signers open the "Multisig orders" menu item in their SKM instance, load the job, and sign.
steps.three: Step 3: The second signer opens the "Multisig orders" menu item in their SKM instance, loads the job, and signs.
steps.four: Step 4: Once enough signatures have been collected, the transaction is sent or can be sent.

local.title: Enter all signatures locally & send directly.
local.desc: Only use if all secret keys can be securely entered on this device (Tests/Recovery/Admin).
local.warning: Warning: All secret keys are being collected in this session. Use only on trusted devices.

infoBody.one: You enter all the required secret keys on this device sequentially until the threshold is reached.
infoBody.two: This is no longer a distributed multisig flow, but "multi-key on one device" – only use this on trusted devices.
infoBody.three: Once enough signatures have been collected, the transaction is sent directly to Horizon (without a multisig job).

xdr.title: Export XDR (do not send)
xdr.desc: Generate a raw XDR transaction for offline tools or hardware wallets.
xdr.short: Export the transaction as XDR without sending it directly.
xdr.infoTitle: How does the XDR export work?

infoBody.one: XDR is the raw transaction format of Stellar. Nothing is automatically sent.
infoBody.two: Use XDR for hardware wallets, offline computers, or other wallets/tools that can import XDR.
infoBody.three: You are responsible for signing and/or sending the XDR later (e.g., via Multi-signature jobs or an external tool).

steps.one: An XDR is generated, but nothing is sent.
steps.two: You can sign the XDR externally (using an offline computer, a hardware wallet, or another tool).
steps.three: To send, you need to re-import the XDR (e.g., via multisig jobs or another tool) and sign/send it there.

job.title: Transaction prepared for multisig
job.status: Multisig job was created. The transaction has not been sent yet.
job.infoTitle: What needs to be done now

info.share: Forward the job ID or XDR to the next signatories (via email or messaging apps such as Telegram or WhatsApp).
info.sign: Signers either choose option 3 ("Import XDR") and sign the XDR, OR open the "Job ID" menu item in the SKM, load the job, and sign there.
info.submit: Once enough signatures have been collected, one of the signers finally sends the job to the network in the SKM (menu "Job ID").

sent.title: Transaction sent
sent.status: Transaction successfully sent to the network.

xdr.title: XDR-ready (not sent)
xdr.status: Only one XDR was generated. Nothing was sent to the network.
xdr.hint: Forward the XDR to other signers or tools (e.g., hardware wallet, offline computer). To send it, import the XDR (signed, if applicable) back into the SKM later (e.g., Option 3) and send it to the network.

confirm.testModeDescription: Existing flow: Enter secret key, gather all required signatures, and send directly to Horizon.
confirm.prepareDescription: Only prepare XDR for production multisig. Nothing will be sent immediately.

jobCreated: Multisig job created (ID: {{id}}). Open "Multisig orders" to see the status.

errors.mismatchedHash: The signed XDR does not belong to this transaction.
errors.signersUnavailable: The account's list of signers is currently unavailable in Horizon. The signature was not applied. Please try again in a few seconds.

prepare.title: Transaction prepared for multisig
prepare.subtitle: The transaction is constructed and can be signed externally.
prepare.notSentHint: This transaction has not yet been sent to the network.
prepare.closeHint: You can close this dialog once you have signed and the multisig order has been created; the next signer will find the Job ID in the “Multisig orders” menu.

testMode.modalDescription: Here, you can enter all the necessary secret keys on one device and send the transaction directly. In real multi-party setups, the keys should remain on separate devices.

banner.title: Productive mode for multisignature
banner.body: In this mode, the Stellar Account Manager uses only your own secret key. Additional signatures are collected via exported XDR files. Secure your XDR files and share them only with trusted signers.

detail.title: Pending multisig job
detail.importSignedXdrDescription: Insert a signed XDR. New signatures will be included in the current transaction.

signatures.title: Signer & Status
signatures.noSessionSecret: No secret key stored in the browser. Please enter the secret or import signed XDR.

xdr.title: Transaction (XDR)
xdr.hint: This XDR can be loaded and signed by other signers in compatible wallets.
xdr.copy.error: XDR could not be copied to the clipboard.

signedXdr.title: Importing signed XDR
signedXdr.hint: Insert an XDR signed by another signer here to merge the signatures with this multisig job.
signedXdr.label: Signed XDR

success.title: Signatures updated
success.body: The signatures have been successfully merged with this multisig job. Current status: {{status}}.

secretPrompt.title: Enter secret to sign
secretPrompt.hint: Please enter the secret key for account {{account}}.

detail.noAccessToken: No access token available. Please load the account that is a signer of this job first.
detail.notASigner: This account is not an active signer of this job.
detail.noLocalSecretForToken: There is no saved secret for this account in this browser. Please open it using a shared link with an access token, or load the account once using "Remember Secret."

options.title: Signature options for this multisig account

distributed.title: Standard: Distributed Multisig Flow
distributed.body: By default, this session only signs with your own secret key. Additional signatures are supplemented via exported XDR files or a job ID from other signers.

localAll.title: Enter all signatures locally
localAll.body: Select this option only if you control all the private keys associated with this account (e.g., after an account recovery involving multiple signers). The Stellar Account Manager will then collect all signatures locally and send the transaction directly to Horizon.
localAll.checkboxLabel: Enter all signatures locally in this dialogue and send directly.
localAll.warning: Note: If you input all signatures locally, all secret keys will be present in this session. Use this option only on trusted devices.

list.title: Multisig orders
list.noAccount: Please load an account first to view multisig transactions.

filter.mine: Only orders that I still need to sign.
filter.assigned: Also orders where I am a signer.

list.deleteTestnetHint: This will remove all multisig jobs on the testnet. Please type "DELETE TESTNET JOBS" to confirm.

success.title: Multisig transaction created
success.body: The multisig transaction with ID {{id}} has been created. Additional signers can sign the XDR file once it has been exported.

statusHelp.pending: The multisig transaction has been created, but there are no signatures yet.
statusHelp.partiallySigned: Some signatories have already signed. More signatures are still needed.
statusHelp.readyToSubmit: There are enough signatures present. The transaction can be sent to Horizon.
statusHelp.submitted: The transaction has been sent to the network. Check the final balance if necessary.
statusHelp.failed: An error occurred while sending the transaction. Please refer to the error log for details.
statusHelp.pending_signatures: The multisig transaction is still awaiting signatures.
statusHelp.ready_to_submit: There are enough signatures. The order can be sent.
